findings.claim: Arbitrary `from` parameter lets an attacker steal tokens from any address that has approved the bridge, minting L2 tokens to themselves.

route.rationale: Theft via arbitrary from is an exploit class but no listed invariant tests it; only withdraw-replay is available, so escalate.

verification.note: no formal tool fit — escalated to human

findings.claim: Anyone can deposit vault tokens on the vault's own behalf (from=vault) to infinitely mint L2 tokens since the vault has approved the bridge.

route.rationale: Supply-exceeds-backing exploit, but no check_ invariant tests minting/backing here, so escalate.

verification.note: no formal tool fit — escalated to human

findings.claim: The arbitrary `target` and `data` call allows a signed message to invoke `transferFrom` from the vault to drain all funds, and signatures can be replayed indefinitely with no nonce.

route.rationale: Arbitrary target/data drain is an exploit class but no listed invariant tests this call abuse; withdraw-replay doesn't cover it, so escalate.

verification.note: no formal tool fit — escalated to human

findings.claim: Withdrawal signatures lack a nonce/replay protection, allowing the same valid signature to be reused to repeatedly drain the vault.

route.rationale: Withdrawal signature replay is directly tested by check_withdrawCannotBeReplayed.
